channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: World of Warcraft's First Official Pride Event Seemingly Arrives in June
item.content:encoded:
According to a new report, the first official World of Warcraft Pride event is set to take place in June, and it'll make a long-running community event that raises money for charity official.

As reported by Wowhead, datamining suggests that the community Running of the Trolls event, which has taken place every year for the last twelve years on the Feathermoon server, will now be made official in the form of the Darkspear Dash.

The event will begin on the Echo Isles, which is where Troll players start their journey, and a parade will then proceed from there to Silvermoon City, where it will culminate in an "extravagant pool party", as Wowhead puts it.

The Running of the Trolls event was originally created by World of Warcraft community members to support The Trevor Project, a charity that provides suicide prevention and mental health support services for LGBTQ+ individuals. Presumably, Blizzard's version of the event will also support this charity.

You'll be able to get your hands on a brand new and unique rainbow-themed toy from the event, and trading post activities will also be available pertaining to the Darkspear Dash, so if you're looking to join in the festivities, there's ample reason to do so.

Supposedly, the Darkspear Dash event will be running between June 27th and 29th as part of Pride month, but nothing official has been revealed by Blizzard yet, so we'll have to wait for confirmation from the developer on this one.

News of World of Warcraft's first official Pride event comes just a month or so after the release of the game's latest expansion, Midnight, which raises the MMO's level cap to 90 (again) and introduces player housing, among other new mechanics and features.

Blizzard is in good company for its first official Pride event; it joins the likes of Poppy Playtime's Mob Entertainment and Temtem's Crema in providing support to a group increasingly in the line of fire in today's fragmenting world.

Assuming Wowhead's datamining is correct, World of Warcraft's Darkspear Dash Pride event begins on June 27th. The MMO is available right now on PC, along with its latest expansion Midnight.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/world-of-warcraft-pride-event-june
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PUBG: Blindspot Officially Shuts Down After Less Than Two Months
item.content:encoded:
Krafton has announced that it's shutting down top-down tactical shooter PUBG: Blindspot less than two months after the game first landed on Steam in Early Access.

Blindspot was released on February 5th, but over the weekend, Sequoia Yang of developer ARC Team announced the game's shutdown, which was set for the unusually close date of March 30th (that's today, by the way).

By way of a reason for the closure, Yang said that although the team had "been exploring multiple ways" to improve PUBG: Blindspot, the devs found that they were "no longer able to sustainably provide the level of experience [they] set out to deliver through Early Access".

Yang continues by saying that PUBG: Blindspot was "a bold attempt to explore new possibilities within the top-down tactical shooter space", and that ARC intends to "return with new experiences in the future" after taking some time to "regroup".

A quick look at Blindspot's Steam numbers might fill in the blanks here. At launch, the game attracted just over 3,000 players at its peak, but the player count quickly dwindled, with Blindspot reaching a 24-hour peak of just 236 players yesterday.

If PUBG: Blindspot had managed to maintain a few thousand players a day, it's possible that the game may have limped on, but I suppose numbers like that just weren't enough to convince Krafton to continue investing in the game, despite a push for esports inclusion.

Of course, this is also a publisher currently fighting a lawsuit brought by the founders of Subnautica 2 developer Unknown Worlds, so financial risks like Blindspot are probably even more unattractive to Krafton than they'd usually be.

Due to PUBG: Blindspot's rapid delisting, you can't play it on Steam anymore, and even if you happen to have it installed on your machine, the servers are dead forevermore, sadly. We'll have to wait and see what ARC does next.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/pubg-blindspot-shutdown

item.title: Xbox Summer Show and Gears of War: E-Day Direct Date Set
item.content:encoded:
Xbox has announced the date and time for its yearly summer showcase in 2026, and the studio has also revealed that Gears of War: E-Day is getting its own Direct show immediately afterwards.

As announced in an Xbox Wire blog post, the Xbox Games Showcase for summer 2026 will be held on June 7th at 10am Pacific (that's 1pm Eastern and 6pm BST). Xbox is promising "huge news on upcoming titles", as well as "first gameplay looks" from both first-party and third-party games.

You can also expect "new details, gameplay, and insights" for Gears of War: E-Day, which has been relatively quiet since it was announced back in 2024. Could a release date be on the cards? Given that the game is still due to launch this year, it seems almost certain.

Playground Games' Fable reboot is also set to arrive sometime this year, so I think we'll probably hear more about that, too. Wishlist-wise, I'd love to see Hideo Kojima's profoundly weird-looking OD and inXile's promising Clockwork Revolution featured during the show.

Back in May 2024, we learned that Spyro Reignited Trilogy studio Toys for Bob is working on a game in partnership with Xbox, so if that's still on the cards, I'm hoping we get to see that during the summer show (and that it's a colorful throwback 3D platformer as well).

There's a chance we'll learn more about some of the games that were announced during last week's Xbox Partner Preview show too, including the likes of Teyon's Hunter: The Reckoning - Deathwish and Rebellion's Alien Deathstorm.

Presumably, there will also be a few surprises scattered throughout the presentation as well. Expect plenty of the games featured to land on PS5 (and, potentially, Nintendo platforms) in addition to Xbox and PC.

Whatever will be featured during the show, we'll learn more when it airs on June 7th at 10am Pacific/1pm Eastern/6pm BST. You'll be able to watch it via Xbox's YouTube and Twitch channels, as usual.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
